seoTitle: How to Create a Free Jira Account Easily
metaDescription: Sign up for a free Jira Cloud account, claim your workspace domain, and set up your first Scrum or Kanban project without a credit card.
intro: You don't need an enterprise budget to get started with agile tracking. This guide walks through signing up for Atlassian's free Jira tier, claiming a workspace domain, and creating your first project.

steps.heading: Go to the signup page
steps.body: Open your browser, navigate to the official Jira Free product signup page, and click Get It Free.

steps.heading: Authenticate your account
steps.body: Sign up using your Google Workspace account or a business email address to set up your login.

steps.heading: Claim your workspace domain
steps.body: Type a unique name into the site domain field to lock in your Atlassian cloud URL.

steps.heading: Choose a project template
steps.body: Answer the quick setup survey or skip it, then choose the Scrum or Kanban tracking template with team-managed settings for easy setup.

steps.heading: Create your first project
steps.body: Enter a name for your project, click Create, and your live working board will render immediately.

faq.question: Is Jira really free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, Atlassian offers a free tier with up to 10 user seats and full agile tracking capabilities without a credit card required.

faq.question: Can I sign up for Jira with my Google account?
faq.answer: Yes, you can authenticate your Jira signup using your Google Workspace account or a business email address.

faq.question: Should I choose Scrum or Kanban for my first Jira project?
faq.answer: Scrum works well for teams running fixed-length sprints, while Kanban suits continuous flow work — both are available as templates during setup.

faq.question: Can I invite team members while setting up a free Jira account?
faq.answer: Yes, during setup you can add immediate team member invitations before finishing your project configuration.
